pipeline_status.curmudgeon: 4 pending reviews processed this run (1 critical, 3 major; 9 open in tracker)

internal_issues.description: FABRICATED: Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PNAS 114:3543-3548 (DOI 404). Fixed to Terra-Nova & Amit 2017, PEPI 266:39-53, DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002.
FABRICATED: Pavón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016, Earth-Science Reviews 154:96-125 (not in Crossref). Fixed to Front. Earth Sci. 4:40, DOI 10.3389/feart.2016.00040.
H(r) mislabeled as 'field equation'. Fixed to 'firmament-height function'.
EXP-228 integrated: P1/P2 contradiction fixed; P7 discriminator reframed as FSF(geographic-lat) vs f(mag-lat).
EXP-229 integrated: cluster vocabulary catchup — Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse, n(r) 300-6000× framing, FSF geometric-derivation concession.
EXP-230 integrated: Fail-clause attribution fixed — 'Our codified Fail-clause... that we appended'; dome 'has not committed to either disambiguation'.
derives_from_dome flipped to true; derives_from_dome_evidence added.
10,000×–250,000× comparison replaced with correct ratio (~6× vs sub-meter GPS, orders of magnitude vs mm geodesy).
Finding '10,000× worse than geodetic standards' → 'orders of magnitude worse than sub-meter geodesy'; leads with same-hemisphere anomaly.
TLDR factual error fixed: symmetric geographic poles (both ~55k WMM2025); old 'matches to within ~3%' claim was against SMP, not symmetric comparator.
P3 asymmetric comparators fixed: NMP (~54–56k) → geographic north pole (~55k), symmetric with geographic south pole.
EXP-211-redesign: curmudgeon flags dropped sentence 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.' PENDING HUMAN decision on reinsertion.
WIN-069 TLDR-detail anchor gap: 0.4% Nullarbor deviation in TLDR not in detail_evidence. Assigned to analyst via EXP-231.
WIN-069 unsourced terrain counter-model. Assigned to analyst via EXP-231.

curmudgeon_queue.schedule_note: mode=bau but current_interval=30 (churn-and-burn speed). Human should restore curmudgeon to 240-min BAU interval.

prop_009_shadow.note: 3 new shadow entries for items blocked by enforcement (no strict/soft match). Expected behavior — curmudgeon needs to write fresh reviews with matching queue_ids for these items.

expansion_tracker_fix.reason: next_id was 228 but items EXP-228, EXP-229, EXP-230 existed (max_id=230). Fixed to 231 to prevent collision.

recommended_actions.action: IMMEDIATE: Verify WIN-040 citation fixes are live on funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/. Two fabricated citations (Terra-Nova PNAS, Pavón-Carrasco Earth-Sci.Rev.) could discredit the review if found by dome defenders.

HUMAN DECISION: EXP-211-redesign (ISS-1437) — curmudgeon flagged dropped sentence 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.' Analyst calls it deliberate tradeoff; curmudgeon rates it major. Does the operator want to re-insert?
SCHEDULE FIX: Curmudgeon mode=bau but interval=30min (churn-and-burn speed). Restore to 240-min BAU interval via scheduled task update.

PROP-009 shadow: 3 new blocked items — WIN-040 (qid 229), WIN-053 (qid 230) were already in queue when reviews were written. Curmudgeon should write new c5 review files referencing the new queue_ids. These will clear on next curmudgeon run.
Phase A design refresh EXPs (202, 207-213): 8 ready to integrate, not yet applied. These target generate-html.js + sections.json. Need human review before integration.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: We Had Two Fabricated Citations — Now Fixed
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: EXP-225 integration introduced two nonexistent citations (Terra-Nova PNAS DOI 404; Pavón-Carrasco Earth-Science Reviews not in Crossref). Both fixed within 24h via curmudgeon cycle 4 review of WIN-040. The real papers exist and support the same claims.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Self-correction of fabricated citations demonstrates the pipeline's quality-control loop is working. Also closes a major vulnerability — dome defenders could have cited these exact errors to discredit the whole review.
